home.description: Computer science student at Epitech Paris and San Francisco State University, passionate about web development, mobile/native, devOps and database systems.

me.description: As a student with a passion for IT, I joined Epitech in October 2020 with a view to obtaining a bachelor's degree in computer science in 2023 and a master in computer science in May 2025. I'm currently on exchange at San Francisco State University for the academic year 2023-2024 and I am currently looking for an internship/job in San Francisco for the summer of 2024 or starting in February 2023. My favorite are web development, front end and back end. I also have skills in DevOps, I'm interested in mobile mobile development, especially on the native language side, and I'm also interested in developing database systems. You can find out more about my projects on my github or on the project page of my portfolio. You can find my resume in the header on the top right.

event.vivatech: In 2022, I had the chance to take part in the Vivatech event as a volunteer and to discover many new technologies.
event.hackaton: In 2022, I had the chance to take part in a Hackaton with Bouygues Telecom to find new ways of using their new API to manage accounts and data. My group and I came up with the idea of creating a Discord bot to simplify data access. We finished 3rd in the hackaton.

timelineElements.epitech: A school with an active pedagogy, no theoretical courses, only projects to learn much faster. You don't learn a language, you learn how to learn, so you can adapt to any situation today, tomorrow or in 10 years' time.
timelineElements.sfsu: A year abroad to improve my English and IT skills and discover a new culture.
timelineElements.carrefour: My first internship was with Carrefour, the world's second-largest distributor. During my internship, I had the chance to work on an internal application as a fullstack developer, creating a storage algorithm for Carrefour's automated warehouses, based on a given list of products.
timelineElements.augeoOne: This internship was part-time, with 3 days at school (Monday-Tuesday-Wednesday) and 2 days at the company (Thursday-Friday). I had the chance to work on the Planzone software as a front-end developer. Mainly on the UI/UX side and to work with the designers in more detail. This internship enabled me to focus on my front-end development and design skills, in order to meet customers' needs.
timelineElements.augeoTwo: This internship, which followed my first internship at Augeo Software, was full-time. Still working as a frontend developer, I mainly worked on the core/logic part of the frontend, managing data between the server and the client. This internship enabled me, after having greatly improved my frontend skills on the design side, to improve on the logic and data management side to better understand the interactions between the client/browser and the server.

projects.rtype: School project at Epitech. Game engine in C++ to create the R-TYPE game and to add network functionalities (online game up to 4 players)
projects.area: School project at Epitech. Creation of a server connected to a database, web and mobile application, all working with docker. The objective is to proprose actions, like receiving an email, and reactions like sending an email or post a tweet and let the user create their own action/reactions
projects.todolist: Todolist server and client, the objective was to deploy the application and try a new software architecture for the client part
projects.handwrittenDigits: Simple server to receive digits image and with a ML algorithm, try to find the digits send. My objective was to make a first ML program.
projects.pokemonCompanion: Application for Android and Wear OS, to make the communication between 2 devices in native language.
projects.database: School project at San Francisco State University. The objective was to create a real database system to answer a real question with all the documentation needed in a company (description, use cases, schema). We create a discord bot to access the data.
projects.softwareEngineering: School project at San Francisco State University. The objective was to create a real web application with a server connected to a database and a client, deploy everything, the final result cannot be on localhost. The application handle all alerts in California for Covid, wild fire, crime and meteo and send alerts to the users in the different counties.
